title: Quiz: Mean-Variance Optimization in Practice
description: Test your understanding of practical portfolio optimization implementation

questions.question: What type of optimization problem is mean-variance portfolio optimization?
questions.explanation: Mean-variance optimization is a quadratic program because the objective (portfolio variance) is quadratic in the weights, while constraints are linear.

questions.question: Why is shrinkage applied to expected return estimates?

options.text: To reduce estimation error and improve stability

questions.explanation: Shrinkage reduces the impact of noisy estimates by pulling them toward a common value, improving out-of-sample performance.

questions.question: What does the Ledoit-Wolf estimator shrink the sample covariance toward?

options.text: A structured target (e.g., diagonal or single-factor model)

questions.explanation: Ledoit-Wolf shrinks toward a structured target matrix, balancing between the noisy sample covariance and a stable but possibly biased target.

questions.question: What problem arises when optimal portfolios are very sensitive to input estimates?

options.text: Small estimation errors cause large weight changes

questions.explanation: High sensitivity means small errors in expected returns or covariances can lead to dramatically different and unstable portfolio weights.

questions.question: Expected returns are easier to estimate reliably than covariances.
questions.explanation: False. Expected returns are much harder to estimate than covariances. Covariances are relatively stable, while detecting statistically significant expected returns requires very long data histories.

questions.question: What is robust optimization designed to handle?
questions.explanation: Robust optimization finds portfolios that perform well across a range of possible input values, accounting for parameter uncertainty.

questions.question: When might quantum computing provide an advantage for portfolio optimization?

options.text: When there are cardinality or integer constraints

questions.explanation: Quantum algorithms like QAOA may provide advantages when constraints make the problem NP-hard, such as cardinality constraints (select k from n) or integer share constraints.

questions.question: Which are common techniques to improve portfolio optimization robustness? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Shrinkage for both returns and covariances, plus weight constraints, all improve robustness. Using only yesterday's data would increase, not decrease, estimation error.
